title: Fumaria saxicola
taxonomy: Fumaria saxicola is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. It belongs to the genus Fumaria, which contains approximately 30 species of annual or biennial plants. The genus Fumaria is classified under the order Ranunculales, which includes flowering plants with four or five petals. Fumaria saxicola is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is also found in some parts of North Africa. This plant is known for its small, yellow or greenish flowers and its ability to thrive in dry, rocky areas.

intro: Fumaria saxicola is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae, commonly known as rock fumitory. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various ailments such as fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Fumaria saxicola include alkaloids, such as fumaritine, fumaritidine, and protopine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
